diagrams.title: Basic Logic Gates with Truth Tables
diagrams.description: Shows the three fundamental logic gates (AND, OR, NOT) with their standard symbols and corresponding truth tables. This diagram helps students visualize how each gate processes binary inputs to produce outputs based on Boolean operations.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental building blocks of all digital circuits and how each gate implements its specific Boolean function

diagrams.title: Boolean Expression to Circuit Conversion
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the conversion of a Boolean expression F = A·B + A' to its corresponding gate-level circuit diagram. Shows how parentheses and operator precedence affect circuit structure. This bridges the gap between algebraic representation and hardware implementation.
diagrams.purpose: Teaches students how to translate Boolean algebra expressions into actual digital circuit implementations

diagrams.title: Half Adder Circuit
diagrams.description: Shows a Half Adder circuit that adds two 1-bit binary numbers. Displays both the circuit diagram using XOR and AND gates, along with the complete truth table showing Sum and Carry outputs. Demonstrates practical application of Boolean algebra in arithmetic operations.
elements: Truth table with all 4 input combinations
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how Boolean algebra principles are applied to create practical digital arithmetic circuits
